title: MCLN EX97 Modify iLPN Traceability

Alex Jones 11/04/2024 0.1 Adding technical note for required DB fields for putaway Brian Brazon 12/10/2024 1.0 Changed EXPIRATION_DATE date pu ll from LPN _DETAIL .EXPIRATION_DATE to LPN.EXPIRATION_DATE EX 97 Modify iLPN Traceability McLane sells certain products at different levels of unit of measure (UOM). For instance, the same product could be sold as a full case, repack or single sell. Due to limitations in the McLane host system, each UOM is tracked under its own unique item number . I f a product sells as case , repack and single sell , inventory is tracked under three item master records. The same is true for freezer vs cooler products- if a single physical item can be stored in either freezer or cooler, ther e will b e two systematic items. While b ase WMOS does not support replenishment across item record s , McLane requires the ability to replenish lower UOM items . This is done by using RF Split Move iLPN to divide a single LPN into two, one for each item, and then using RF Modify iLPN to convert one item into another where needed. Sometimes the user will use RF Modify iLPN without RF Split Move iLPN . If this is the case for an active location, RF Move ILPN Frm Act is used first to move the LPN to status “In inventory, Not putaway.” Changes from one item to another cause errors in McLane’s host systems when data for an LPN is sent with an item that was not associated with that LPN’s ASN. To prevent these errors, WMOS is also enhanced to remove ASN information from any LPN that has its item changed during RF Modify iLPN. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y RF Modify iLPN Reporting N Other N McLane requires the following fields at the LPN and LPN detail level to be retained:Lot , GLN, entry number , scanned barcode, and expiration date. Base WM removes some of these values when an item is changed, but since they are the same physical item for McLane, the attributes will remain the same . Therefore, as part of this extension WMOS is enhanced to allow those attributes to remain associated to an LPN when its item is changed via Ctrl+G , and to remove ASN data from an LPN at the same time . Assumptions Base item substitution logic is disabled with this extension and not supported in parallel with this extension. No changes to the RF or UI screen flows are in scope for this extension. All iLPN inventory is single SKU. Any multi-SKU iLPNs are manually broken down to single SKU prior to custom iLPN modification. All inventory in WMOS is stored in units. This is applicable to each of the McLane UOMs of case, repack and single sell. One WMOS unit of case item is equivalent to one case. One WMOS unit of repack item is equivalent to one repack. One WMOS unit of single sell item is equivalent to one single sell. Modify iLPN is eligible to be used for LPNs in active locations with status In inventory, Not putaway and in reserve locations with status In inventory, Not putaway or Putaway. Data fields tracked by item per base (lot , item attributes, and expiration date) can be configured to never prompt, always prompt , or prompt if null in item inventory tracking. Transaction parameters for RF Modify iLPN aside from 3-3: Print Label on iLPN Modification will not be changed. Specifically, 1-2: (null), 4-4: No, 5-5: (null), 6-6: (null), 7-7: No, and 8-9: (null) No validations for item quantity, reason code, reference number, or any other fields entered as part of RF Split Move iLPN or RF Modify iLPN are added or changed. Lot/item validations will not be enforced while EX97 is active. A lot does not need to be associated with an item to be applied to an LPN containing that item. When an LPN is changed from one item to another, it will be changed to an item configured with the same traceability requirements. No changes are made to Modify LPN for oLPNs. a ll changes are restricted to iLPNs . LPNs that have been unlocated must be put away before they can be allocated. No changes to WMOS messages are in scope for this extension. Any LPN that is modified to contain a different item while in In Inventory, Not Putaway status has an ASN already associated with it. Once the ASN is removed from the LPN, users will not be able to view that LPN as being associated with the ASN directly through the UI or database. The modified LPN will not be associated with the ASN through the ASNs UI or through any other UI using an ASN filter. Alternate methods for finding the ASN associated with an LPN are provide d through both the UI and through the database. Any LPN that has its item changed via Modify iLPN will have its traceability fields retained and ASN fields removed. Modify iLPN process for an LPN that does not have its item changed is not affected by EX97 processes. Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow Process Step s for LPNs in Active locations Enter Move ILPN Frm Act transaction, or proceed to #2 Enter LPN Enter whether location is now empty Transaction is completed Enter Split Move iLPN transaction , unless #1 was completed Enter LPN Enter quantity to be moved Enter new LPN to be created, or use system generated LPN Transaction is completed Enter Modify iLPN transaction Enter iLPN # Use Ctrl+G to indicate a change in item Enter updated quantity Enter reason code . Valid reason codes are configured in system code B-051 Inventory Adjustments. Enter reference number Transaction is completed Process Steps for LPNs in Reserve locations (Optional) Enter Split Move iLPN transaction Enter LPN Enter quantity to be moved Enter new LPN to be created, or use system generated LPN Transaction is completed Enter Modify iLPN transaction Enter iLPN # Use Ctrl+G to indicate a change in item Enter updated quantity Enter reason code. Valid reason codes are configured in system code B-051 Inventory Adjustments. Enter reference number Transaction is completed UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. The below methods can be used by McLane end users to find the ASN associated with an LPN after it has been removed from the LPN table: Shipments UI: Filtering by Shipment and then viewing LPNs associated with that Shipment will show all LPNs associated with that shipment as well as ASN for any unmodified LPNs. iLPNs UI: Filtering by Shipment will show all LPNs associated with that shipment as well as ASN for any unmodified LPNs. ASNs UI: Filtering by Shipment will allow end users to find the relevant ASN for that shipment without the LPNs being directly associated with the ASN. Note that users will not see any LPN formerly associated with an ASN through the ASNs UI or through any other UI when filtering by ASN. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF RF Modify iLPN & Split Move iLPN Traceability As a part of inventory management, u sers have the ability to use RF Split Move iLPN to split and move an iLPN before modifying it. In that process, users take a single iLPN and split it into two iLPNs , the newly created one of which is remo v ed from its location. This process is modified so that the following fields at the LPN level are unchanged and remain as they were prior to iLPN modification: ASN, Lot, GLN, entry number, scanned barcode, and expiration date. iLPN processing after modification is not enhanced and is as per base process. Technical note: Value not currently retained per base is limited to the following: lpn lpn_detai l.expiration _date U sers also have the ability to use RF Modify iLPN to change characteristics of an iLPN . In that process, users have the ability to use Ctrl+G to change the LPN from one item to another. This process is modified so that the following fields at the LPN level are unchanged and remain as they were prior to iLPN modification: ASN, Lot, GLN, entry number, scanned barcode, and expiration date . iLPN processing after modification is not enhanced and is as per base process. Technical note: Values that should be retained are as follows: lpn _detail .batch_nbr , lpn_ detail. item _attr_2 (GLN), lpn_ detail.item _attr_3 ( entry number ), lpn_detail.ref_field_7 (scanned barcode) , lpn _detail .expiration_date , Technical note 2: Modifying an LPN also requires that wm_inventory be updated with the same traceability fields to prevent putaway errors. The applicable fields are lot ( batch_nbr ), GLN (item_attr_2), and entry number (item_attr_3). Barcode and expiration date are not stored in wm_inventory and require no changes within that tab l e. ASN Association RF Modify iLPN Is also modified so that when Ctrl+G is used to change the LPN from one item to another , ASN fields are removed from the modified LPN. This functionality has its own enablement separate from traceability enablement. Messages are not enhanced and instead will only be affected by the lack of ASN information associated with the LPN. Technical note: Values removed when Ctrl+G is used are lpn.asn_id and lpn.tc_asn_id . This update should take place immediately after the RF Modify iLPN transaction is completed. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX 97 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 97 is turned off, and no custom EX 97 processing will occur. Third, the transaction specific flag for RF Split Move iLPN: Traceability must be enabled using custom transaction parameter byte 500-500. This parameter is configured as a Y/B flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing detailed in 7.1.1: Traceability. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX97 traceability is turned off, and no custom EX97 traceability processing will occur. Fourth , the transaction specific flag for RF Modify iLPN : Traceability must be enabled using custom transaction parameter byte 50 0 -50 0 . This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing detailed in 7 .1.1: Traceability . Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 97 traceability is turned off, and no custom EX 97 traceability processing will occur. Finally, the transaction specific flag for RF Modify iLPN: ASN Association must be enabled using custom transaction parameter byte 501-501. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing detailed in 7 .1.2: ASN Association. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX97 ASN association is turned off, and no custom EX97 traceability processing related to removing ASN data from the LPN table will occur. Outputs Activity Tracking No changes to base behavior for Activity Tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to base behavior for PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks No changes to base behavior for labor management as part of this extension. Process Change/Addition No process changes or additions in scope for this extension. Reporting , Label, or Document No report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. Menu Parameters Parameter (Category > Parameter) Value Warehouse Master Parameter (Tab > Section > Parameter ) Value System Codes Code ID Details Parameter Configuration Rec Type Code Type Code ID Short Description Description Frm To Description Validation Type Configured value C 000 000 Global Extension Control Global Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – Global Extension Control is turned ON C 000 EX97 EX97 Extension Control EX97 Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – EX97 Extension Control is turned ON Program Parameters Program   From Byte   To Byte   Description   Parameter Required   Parameter Config   RF Modify iLPN   5 0 0  5 0 0  EX97 Traceability N Check Box    Y/N Flag   RF Modify iLPN 501 501 EX97 ASN Association N Check Box    Y/N Flag   Database Not required for this extension. The below query can be used by McLane during report building or for any other purpose to find the ASN associated with an LPN after it has been removed from the LPN table: select a.tc_asn_id , a.tc_shipment_id , l.tc_lpn_id , l.* from asn a, lpn l, shipment s where a.shipment _id = s.shipment _id and l.shipment _id = s.shipment _id and tc_lpn_id =' '; Test Cases General C onditions Condition Name Description and expectations Active and reserve location classes Single and multiple LPNs previously located in location Item attribute config never prompt, always, and if null Reserve location with and without Split Move iLPN Multiple splits, multiple modifications Full green path flow including allocat ing modified iLPN Printing t ransaction parameter config uration for RF Modify iLPN set to all possible values Active location with Move ILPN Frm Act or Split Move iLPN transactions ASN is removed from an iLPN in In Inventory, Not Putaway status ASN is removed from an iLPN in Putaway status Exceptions Condition Name Description and expectations Questions and Answers This below table is used to track questions that were asked and answered regarding this enhancement. If the question resulted in any action it is listed against the question. # Asked By Question Answered By Answer Date Added Action Taken 1 2 3 4

Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX97 ASN association is turned off, and no custom EX97 traceability processing related to removing ASN data from the LPN table will occur. The below query can be used by McLane during report building or for any other purpose to find the ASN associated with an LPN after it has been removed from the LPN table: select a.tc_asn_id , a.tc_shipment_id , l.tc_lpn_id , l.* from asn a, lpn l, shipment s where a.shipment _id = s.shipment _id and l.shipment _id = s.shipment _id and tc_lpn_id =' '; Test Cases General C onditions Condition Name Description and expectations Active and reserve location classes Single and multiple LPNs previously located in location Item attribute config never prompt, always, and if null Reserve location with and without Split Move iLPN Multiple splits, multiple modifications Full green path flow including allocat ing modified iLPN Printing t ransaction parameter config uration for RF Modify iLPN set to all possible values Active location with Move ILPN Frm Act or Split Move iLPN transactions ASN is removed from an iLPN in In Inventory, Not Putaway status ASN is removed from an iLPN in Putaway status Exceptions Condition Name Description and expectations Questions and Answers This below table is used to track questions that were asked and answered regarding this enhancement.
